content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 3, 2013

Sarah Carrillo
County Counsel
2 South Green Street
Sonora, California 95370

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-13-085

Dear Ms. Carrillo:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding your agency's Conflict of Interest Code under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ After our conversation on July 3, 2013, you agreed to the withdrawal of your request for advice. Also, as we discussed, we have forwarded your request for review to the proper party within our agency and your advice request has been withdrawn.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

By: [Signature] Heather M. Rowan
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

HMW:jgl

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought advice regarding a county agency's Conflict of Interest Code under the Political Reform Act, but subsequently agreed to withdraw the request.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed withdrawal of the advice request and noted that the code review request has been forwarded to the appropriate party within the agency.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. Document confirms mutual agreement to withdraw after July 3, 2013 conversation. Request for code review was referred to appropriate FPPC division.

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal letter where the FPPC confirms that Sarah Carrillo, County Counsel, agreed to withdraw her request for advice regarding her agency's Conflict of Interest Code. The FPPC has forwarded the code review request to the proper internal party.
